name: Middy (Node.js Lambda Middleware Framework)

auth.notes: Lambda middleware; uses execution role for AWS services

performance.notes: Middleware chain overhead negligible; designed for Lambda

agent_readiness.notes: Middleware engine for Node.js Lambda. middy(handler).use(middleware). Official middleware: @middy/http-json-body-parser (parse body). @middy/ssm (SSM parameters). @middy/secrets-manager. @middy/event-normalizer. @middy/validator (JSON Schema). @middy/http-error-handler. @middy/sqs-partial-batch-failure. @middy/warmup. Express-like before/after/onError hooks. TypeScript support. Reduces boilerplate in Lambda handlers.

gotchas: v5 has breaking changes from v4 (no CommonJS default)
Middleware ordering matters for before/after hooks
Each middleware is a separate package install
Lambda cold start slightly increased with many middlewares
best_when: Node.js Lambda functions needing consistent middleware (auth, logging, validation)
API Gateway Lambda with body parsing, validation, error handling
SQS Lambda consumers with partial batch failure handling
avoid_when: Python Lambda (use AWS Lambda Powertools instead)
Simple one-liner Lambda functions (overhead not worth it)
summary: Middy is the Node.js Lambda middleware framework. middy(handler).use(middleware). Official middlewares: JSON body parsing, SSM parameters, Secrets Manager, input validation, error handling, SQS partial batch failure. Express-like before/after/onError hooks. TypeScript support. Reduces Lambda handler boilerplate. The Node.js Lambda equivalent of AWS Lambda Powertools.
